title: SignalGuard AI — Channel × Audience Adaptation Matrix
description: Lookup table for the Adapter agent. Maps each channel and audience combination to specific formatting, tone, and compliance requirements.

SMS.format: Single block text. No line breaks unless critical. Abbreviations allowed for space (e.g., 'w/', 'elig.', 'cond.', 'mo.').
SMS.cta_format: Short URL only — no button text, no 'Click here'
SMS.disclosure_rules: All key conditions must fit within 160 chars or link to terms page. Never split across multiple SMS.

Consumer (General).tone: Brief, clear, savings-first. No jargon. Plain language abbreviations OK.
Consumer (General).example: iPhone 17 Pro $0/mo w/ elig. trade-in on Unlimited Premium ($85.99/mo). 36-mo credits. novatel.com/t Msg&Data rates apply STOP to opt out

Executive (VP+).tone: Not recommended for executive audience — SMS is too informal for VP-level outreach. Use email or LinkedIn instead.

Small Business Owner.tone: Direct, cost-focused. Lead with per-line price.
Small Business Owner.example: NovaTel Business: $30/line 5+ lines w/ AutoPay. Unlimited data + 15GB hotspot. novatel.com/biz Msg&Data rates apply STOP to opt out

Technical (IT/Ops).tone: Not recommended — SMS lacks the space for technical detail.

Email.format: Subject line ≤ 60 chars. 2-3 sentences per paragraph. Max 4 paragraphs in body. Bullet points for features.
Email.cta_format: Button-style CTA from approved list + tracked URL
Email.disclosure_rules: Key conditions near offer headline. Full disclosure block at bottom. Both required.

Consumer (General).tone: Friendly, conversational, savings-forward. Explain bill credits in plain language. Assume reader doesn't know wireless jargon.

Executive (VP+).tone: Concise, outcome-focused. Lead with business impact. No exclamation marks. Data-backed claims only.

Small Business Owner.tone: Practical, cost-transparent. Lead with 'what it costs and what you get.' Acknowledge they don't have time for sales pitches.

Technical (IT/Ops).tone: Specification-led. Can use technical terms (QoS, SASE, mmWave). Include links to technical documentation.

LinkedIn.format: 1-2 sentences per paragraph with white space between. No wall-of-text. Professional but not stiff.
LinkedIn.cta_format: Professional CTAs: 'See how it works', 'Compare plans', 'Book a demo'
LinkedIn.disclosure_rules: Disclosures visible in post body — not hidden below 'See More' fold.

Consumer (General).tone: Not typical for LinkedIn consumer content — reframe as 'smart consumer' angle if needed.

Executive (VP+).tone: Strategic, concise. Lead with industry trend → NovaTel's role. No hard sell. Thought-leadership framing.

Small Business Owner.tone: Empathetic, practical. 'We know you're busy' framing. Cost predictability emphasis.

Technical (IT/Ops).tone: Peer-to-peer. Can discuss spectrum, architecture, APIs. Link to whitepapers or tech briefs.

Facebook/Instagram.format: Short, scannable. Line breaks between ideas. Emoji allowed (≤ 3 per post). Hashtags optional (≤ 3).
Facebook/Instagram.cta_format: Platform CTA buttons ('Shop now', 'Learn more') — NOT in copy text. In-copy CTA from approved list.
Facebook/Instagram.disclosure_rules: ALL material disclosures MUST be visible without 'See More' click. Instagram Stories: disclosures on same frame as claim.

Consumer (General).tone: Friendly, visual-forward, savings-focused. Short sentences. Emoji to break up text (but ≤ 3).

Executive (VP+).tone: Not recommended for executive audience on Facebook/Instagram.

Small Business Owner.tone: Relatable, 'small biz owner to small biz owner' angle. Before/after cost comparisons work well.

Technical (IT/Ops).tone: Not recommended for technical audience on Facebook/Instagram.

Landing_Page.format: Headline ≤ 10 words. 2-3 sentences per section. Subheadings for scanability. Plan comparison table if multiple plans.
mandatory_elements: Broadband Facts label link (if speed claims made)
Landing_Page.cta_format: Primary: 'Shop now' or 'Switch today'. Secondary: 'Compare plans'. Button-style.
Landing_Page.disclosure_rules: Disclosures in SAME VIEWPORT as triggering claim. Separate T&C section at bottom is NOT sufficient for above-fold claims.

Consumer (General).tone: Clear, benefit-led. Short hero text, feature bullets, prominent CTA. Explain everything in plain language.

Executive (VP+).tone: ROI-focused hero section. Case study or data proof point immediately visible. Clean design, minimal text.

Small Business Owner.tone: Cost-comparison led. 'What you pay vs. what you get' layout. FAQ section addressing common SMB concerns.

Technical (IT/Ops).tone: Spec sheet format. Feature table with technical details. API documentation links. Architecture diagrams referenced.

Press_Release.format: AP Style. Inverted pyramid. 2-3 sentences per paragraph. FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE header.
Press_Release.cta_format: NO CTA in body. End with 'For more information, visit novatel.com'.
Press_Release.disclosure_rules: Financial claims need 'as of [date]' qualifier. Speed/coverage claims need source citation inline.

Media.tone: Factual, newsworthy angle first. Quotes from executives. No marketing language — AP style neutrality.

Investor.tone: Forward-looking statement required. Financial metrics sourced. Cautious language on projections.
